provenance.subject: Mathematics
provenance.chapterTitle: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.text: A pair of linear equations in two variables can have:
questions.explanation: A pair of linear equations can have: (i) unique solution (intersecting lines), (ii) no solution (parallel lines), (iii) infinite solutions (coincident lines).

questions.text: If two lines are parallel, they have no solution.
questions.explanation: Parallel lines never intersect, so there is no common point that satisfies both equations. Hence, no solution.

questions.text: What is the condition for coincident lines?
questions.explanation: For coincident lines (infinite solutions), the ratios of corresponding coefficients must be equal: a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ = c₁/c₂

questions.text: The graph of a linear equation in two variables is a ________.
questions.explanation: A linear equation in two variables (ax + by + c = 0) always represents a straight line when graphed.
